query: What is the definition of a Chartered Student Organization (CSO) according to this policy?
document: Chartered Student Organizations (CSOs) are defined by Regents Policy, Chapter 7, RP 7

query: What is the definition of a Chartered Student Organization (CSO) according to this policy?
document: Collectively, student governance groups of the University are known as chartered student organizations (CSOs) and are advised and operationally supported by University-assigned faculty or staff typically in a student life / student activities office

query: What is the definition of a Chartered Student Organization (CSO) according to this policy?
document:
Definitions

“CSO” means a campus-wide student association organized to carry out functions
or operations on behalf of the university for the purpose of serving the
entire student body on its campus

query: What is the definition of a Chartered Student Organization (CSO) according to this policy?

Definitions

“CSO” means a campus-wide student association organized to carry out functions
or operations on behalf of the university for the purpose of serving the
entire student body on its campus

query: What is the definition of a Chartered Student Organization (CSO) according to this policy?
document:
A CSO is a campus-wide student association organized to carry out
functions or operations on behalf of the university for the purpose of serving
the entire student body on its campus

query: How are the activities of a CSO financed?
document:
A CSO’s activities are directly financed through mandatory student
activity fees, as authorized by Section 304A-2257, HRS

query: According to section III.B.1, how does the university officially recognize a CSO in relation to the university's academic programs?

the student life co-curricular program that complements the regular
instructional program, but is not a department of the university

query: In the case of a forged UHGA check, what documentation must the payee provide to the General Accounting and Loan Collection Office?
document:
If the payment status of the check is not stated as “Cleared Check”,
complete the Stop Payment/Cancellation/Reissue Form: GALC-625 (Attachment 1),
and submit the original completed form to the General Accounting and Loan
Collection Office with a screen shot of the “Search for Payment” query as the
supporting documentation

query: In the case of a forged UHGA check, what documentation must the payee provide to the General Accounting and Loan Collection Office?
document:
Have payee return
all three (3) notarized sets along with a police report number to the General
Accounting and Loan Collection Office

query: What happens to funds from a cancelled check issued from prior-year general funds?
document:
Cancellation of a check from prior year general funds reverts to the State
Treasury

query: What happens to funds from a cancelled check issued from prior-year general funds?
document:
For general fund accounts (UH Appropriations G011 to G3xx), monies received
for a prior year expenditure shall lapse to the State general fund

query: What happens to funds from a cancelled check issued from prior-year general funds?
document:
A check may need to be cancelled (voided) for various
reasons, such as errors in the payee name, amount, or other discrepancies

query: What happens to funds from a cancelled check issued from prior-year general funds?
document:
If the payment status of the check is not stated as “Cleared Check”,
complete the Stop Payment/Cancellation/Reissue Form: GALC-625 (Attachment 1),
and submit the original completed form to the General Accounting and Loan
Collection Office with a screen shot of the “Search for Payment” query as the
supporting documentation

query: According to this document, what is considered a "stale dated check"?
document:
Stale dated checks - Checks issued by the University that have not been
cashed with six (6) months from the date of the check

query: To whom is the authority to establish parking procedures on individual campuses delegated?
document:
Delegation of Authority

The President is delegated the authority to promulgate practices and
procedures governing parking and operation of motor vehicles on University
campuses

query: To whom is the authority to establish parking procedures on individual campuses delegated?
document:
Delegation of Authority

The President is delegated the authority to promulgate practices and
procedures governing parking and operation of motor vehicles on University
campuses

query: To whom is the authority to establish parking procedures on individual campuses delegated?
document:
In consideration of the uniqueness of each campus, the President
delegates authority to Chancellors to promulgate practices and procedures to
govern parking and operation of motor vehicles on the Chancellor’s respective
campus in the context of Title 20, Chapter 12, Parking and Operations of Motor
Vehicles, Hawai‘i Administrative Rules

query: To whom is the authority to establish parking procedures on individual campuses delegated?
document:
In consideration of the uniqueness of each campus, the President
delegates authority to Chancellors to promulgate practices and procedures to
govern parking and operation of motor vehicles on the Chancellor’s respective
campus in the context of Title 20, Chapter 12, Parking and Operations of Motor
Vehicles, Hawai‘i Administrative Rules

query: To whom is the authority to establish parking procedures on individual campuses delegated?
document:
Purpose

To codify the delegation of authority to the President to promulgate practices
and procedures governing parking and operation of motor vehicles on University
campuses

query: What is the purpose of Executive Policy 10.501?
document:
The purpose of this executive policy is to establish guidelines
for the identification of high demand disciplines

query: What is the purpose of Executive Policy 10.501?
document:
Purpose

This Executive Policy maintains the policy-based administrative infrastructure
created by former Board policy relating to the designation of high demand
disciplines

query: What is the purpose of Executive Policy 10.501?
document:

To provide procedural and suggested processes to
implement Executive Policy, E9

using the same object code

document:
REX to general funds shall be recorded in the same fiscal 
year that funds were spent

account which are recovered in a subsequent fiscal year

query: What specific actions are considered workplace violence under this policy, according to Section III.A?
document:

of discrimination or harassment or for assisting or testifying in an investigation or 
complaint resolution pursuant to this policy, as discussed in further detail in Section 
III

query: What specific actions are considered workplace violence under this policy, according to Section III.A?
document:
The University of Hawai‘i prohibits any work related or workplace violence
against its students, faculty, staff, visitors and contract employees which
materially and substantially interferes with an individual’s work, academic
performance, and/or workplace safety and/or otherwise subjectively and
objectively creates a hostile environment

query: If a student experiences a threatening incident on campus, to whom should they report it, and what section of the policy outlines this procedure?
document:
Executives, managers, and supervisors are expected to report incidents to 
the campus Coordinator, or other designee when they are notified or 
otherwise become aware of conduct that may constitute discrimination, 
harassment, or retaliation as defined in this policy

query: If a student experiences a threatening incident on campus, to whom should they report it, and what section of the policy outlines this procedure?
document:
Students, visitors, and contract employees should report incidents to the
appropriate dean, administrator, or respective campus security personnel who
will take appropriate action, in accordance with statutes and University
policies
